intro_md:
At a club the size of Carmel, indirect costs are spread across every department and hundreds of routine purchasing decisions, renewing with little independent market validation.

Rarely is this obvious overspending — costs quietly drift upward, eroding the operating surplus that funds reinvestment, capital improvements, and the member experience.

reasons.title: A Fragmented Vendor Base
reasons.body: Golf, grounds, dining, and facilities each source on their own. No single function sees total volume — so the club never buys as the $6M-plus purchaser it is, and rates drift above market.

reasons.title: Inherited, Auto-Renewing Terms
reasons.body: Agreements renew on incumbent terms — rebates, escalators, and fees set years ago. The value is recovered by re-testing the contract you hold, not by switching vendors.

reasons.title: A Member-Funded Cost Base
reasons.body: FY2024 revenue of $36.5M produced a $3.9M operating surplus. Every dollar of recurring indirect cost is dues pressure or reinvestment forgone — making sourcing a matter of stewardship.

reasons.title: Food & Beverage Is Not Disclosed
reasons.body: The Form 990 doesn't break out food & beverage — usually a club's single largest member-facing cost. Built from disclosed lines only, this understates the true base; F&B is sized directly in the baseline.

opportunity.basis_note: From Carmel's FY2024 Form 990 and ERA category benchmarks; validated in the 30-day baseline review.

observations: Operating supply (~$2.0M) is the largest disclosed line — concentrated enough that re-pricing moves real dollars to the surplus.
Spend spans six-plus departments with no central procurement, so pricing fragments and savings go unclaimed.
Food & beverage isn't disclosed, so the true addressable base — and the recoverable opportunity — is larger than shown.
The 990 shows what Carmel spends, not what it contracted or when terms were last tested — the gap is where value sits.

categories.name: Operating Supply
categories.why_md: One of the largest controllable operating categories. Bought across many vendors, it is where consolidation and an independent market test produce the most recoverable surplus.

categories.name: Professional Services
categories.why_md: Professional-service agreements often renew untouched for years — making both scope and rate among the highest-return targets for independent benchmarking.

categories.name: Office Supplies
categories.why_md: High purchasing volume and fragmented buying create recurring opportunities for cost recovery — among the fastest, lowest-disruption gains available.

categories.name: Maintenance
categories.why_md: Service agreements renew on incumbent terms across clubhouse and grounds; re-testing scope and pricing at renewal turns routine spend into recurring savings.

categories.name: Utilities
categories.why_md: Tariffs, meter configuration, and billing errors are recurring sources of recovery — independent of usage, so savings reach the surplus without touching operations.

categories.name: Insurance
categories.why_md: Periodic re-marketing tests both the rate and the broker compensation embedded in it — costs that rarely surface on their own but compound year over year.

methodology.recovery_note: blend of ERA category rates; contract-timing haircuts applied at baseline

honesty.cannot_know_md: Built from Carmel's public FY2024 Form 990 and ERA category benchmarks. Contract terms and current pricing are not visible from outside — some lines may be recently bid, others may hold more room than shown. The 990 also omits food & beverage — typically a club's largest member-facing cost — so the true base is likely larger. A starting estimate, not a finding.
honesty.baseline_md: The no-cost 30-day baseline replaces these estimates with actual contract data — across every applicable category, not only the six shown — and quantifies food & beverage, which the filing omits. It confirms where the real room is and where Carmel is already competitive. The result is a finding, not an estimate.

reassurance: No savings, no fee. No upfront cost. 90%+ of incumbent suppliers retained.

source.filing: Carmel Country Club FY2024 IRS Form 990 — Part IX Statement of Functional Expenses
source.basis: Mapped functional-expense lines; direct compensation, depreciation, interest, and taxes excluded.
